title: Zero-G Crystal Growth Dynamics
hypothesis: Crystal lattice structures grown in microgravity will exhibit 40% fewer defects than Earth-grown counterparts, enabling superior semiconductor applications.
findings: Preliminary results show 32% reduction in lattice defects. Further trials underway.

title: Dark Energy Field Interaction Study
hypothesis: Localized dark energy fluctuations near nebula boundaries correlate with measurable graviton emissions detectable by quantum sensors.
findings: Detected anomalous graviton signatures at 3 of 7 survey points. Statistical significance pending.

title: Extremophile Adaptation in Vacuum Conditions
hypothesis: Tardigrade-derived organisms can sustain metabolic activity in hard vacuum for periods exceeding 72 hours when pre-treated with cryoprotectants.
findings: Confirmed: modified tardigrade cultures survived 96 hours in vacuum with 78% viability. Published in Interstellar Journal of Astrobiology.

title: Quantum Entanglement Relay Stability
hypothesis: Quantum-entangled particle pairs can maintain coherence over distances exceeding 500 AU when shielded by magnetohydrodynamic fields.
findings: Coherence maintained at 487 AU. Degradation curve suggests 520 AU theoretical maximum. MHD shielding proved essential above 200 AU.

title: Nebular Gas Composition Mapping
hypothesis: The Forge Nebula contains trace concentrations of stable transuranic elements formed during the preceding supernova event.

title: Artificial Gravity Long-Term Health Effects
hypothesis: Crew members exposed to 0.8g artificial gravity for 6+ months show statistically equivalent bone density to 1g Earth controls.
findings: At month 8, bone density averages 96.2% of Earth baseline. Muscle mass within 3% variance.

title: Solar Wind Plasma Harvesting Efficiency
hypothesis: Magnetic funnel arrays can capture and compress solar wind plasma to generate 15% of station auxiliary power requirements.

title: Micro-Organism Biofilm Formation in Space Alloys
hypothesis: Biofilm formation on titanium-vanadium station hull alloys proceeds 2.3x faster in space radiation environments than in shielded laboratories.
